title: Making plans
setting: Roberto calls you. He wants to make plans for the weekend.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: ¿Qué hacés el sábado? Vamos a la playa.
steps.en: What are you doing Saturday? Let's go to the beach.

options.feedback: Enthusiastic and natural. 'Dale' is the perfect way to say yes here.
Practical — getting the details before committing is totally normal.
True, but it's a flat reply to a fun invite. Show some interest!

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: Salimos temprano, como a las seis de la mañana.
steps.en: We leave early, around six in the morning.

options.feedback: Natural reaction plus agreement — sounds like a real friend chatting.
Clear confirmation of the time. No confusion later.

options.es: Las seis de la tarde, bien.
options.feedback: He said morning (de la mañana)! You'd miss the whole trip.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: Nos vemos en mi casa. No llegués tarde.
steps.en: Meet at my house. Don't be late.

options.feedback: Reassuring and easy — 'tranquilo' is a very Nica way to say no worries.
Not the thing to say after he asked you not to be late!

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: Tuani. Entonces nos vemos el sábado, pues.
steps.en: Cool. So we'll see each other Saturday, then.

options.feedback: Warm sign-off between friends. Exactly the right register.
Casual and friendly — 'ahí nos vemos' is perfect for closing plans.
'Señor'?! Way too formal for a friend. Keep it casual with Roberto.
